prompt: Write four absurd but coherent sentences in the voice of a confused medieval chronicler who has been hired to document the Q3 results of a modern SaaS company.
response:
# Chronicles of the Digital Realm: Q3 Observations

Verily, the mystical vapors known as "cloud services" have swelled our coffers this quarter, though I remain confounded as to how coin materializes from that which cannot be touched by mortal hands. 

The high priests of "Customer Success" have performed strange rituals they call "onboarding," which I suspect involves no actual boards nor ships, yet somehow prevents clients from fleeing our domain.

The lord treasurer speaks of "burn rate" with great concern, which I first mistook for a warning of witch trials, but appears instead to reference gold vanishing at alarming speed whilst producing naught but promises of future abundance.

I am told our "KPIs" are "crushing it," though I see neither indicators being crushed nor evidence of physical destruction, merely colorful circles upon magical glowing tablets that cause the elders of "Venture Capital" to nod with satisfaction.
